title: jasminum zippelianum
intro: Jasminum zippelianum, also known as Zippel's jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, native to the Philippines. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. Its leaves and flowers have been traditionally used to treat fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, offering various health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
